{"query":"Safe Superintelligence SSI March 2025 valuation funding amount raised","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1448,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://news.crunchbase.com/business/biggest-rounds-april-2025-safe-superintelligence-plaid/","title":"The 10 Biggest Rounds Of April: Safe Superintelligence’s $2B Round Leads The Way","content":"Check out March’s biggest rounds here.
Startups in fintech, energy and more all landed big rounds as investors showered some cash in April.
SSI, co-founded by OpenAI‘s former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever, reportedly raised a $2 billion round at a $32 billion valuation led by Greenoaks Capital Partners.
Plaid, $575M, fintech: Plaid raised a $575 million round led by Franklin Templeton at a $6.1 billion valuation.
Silicon Ranch, $500M, energy: Nashville, Tennessee-based Silicon Ranch raised $500 million from European infrastructure investor AIP Management.
Back in 2022, Silicon Ranch — which operates solar and battery projects — raised $775 million in equity capital led by Manulife Investment Management.
Chainguard, $356M, cybersecurity: Software supply chain security startup Chainguard raised a massive $356 million Series D at a valuation of $3.5 billion as companies look for ways to secure software development.
The new valuation is a more than threefold increase from the $1.12 billion valuation the company received after a $140 million Series C led by IVP, Lightspeed Venture Partners and Redpoint.
Supabase, $200M, database: Database developer Supabase raised a $200 million Series D led by Accel at a $2 billion valuation.
We tracked the largest rounds in the Crunchbase database that were raised by U.S.-based companies for the month of April 2025.

The group launched SSI in June 2024, shortly after Sutskever left OpenAI amid a failed internal coup against CEO Sam Altman.
The latest $2 billion haul suggests investors are not only betting on Sutskever’s past track record—he was instrumental in OpenAI’s breakthroughs with GPT—but also on the possibility that SSI will be the next foundational model company to challenge OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google DeepMind."},{"url":"https://salestools.io/en/report/safe-superintelligence-ssi-raises-1-billion-series-a-september-2024","title":"Safe Superintelligence (SSI) Raises $1 | SalesTools AI","content":"Safe Superintelligence (SSI) was founded in 2024 and is headquartered in Palo Alto, California / Tel Aviv, Israel.

The company's mission is to focus on safely developing a superintelligence, a computer-based agent capable of surpassing human intelligence. On May 15, 2024, OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever left OpenAI after a board dispute where he voted to fire Sam Altman amid concerns about communication and trust. On June 19, 2024, Sutskever posted on X that he was starting SSI Inc, with the goal to safely develop superintelligent AI, alongside Daniel Levy, and Daniel Gross. In July 2025, co-founder Gross left the company to join Meta Superintelligence Labs, and Sutskever became the CEO of SSI. 1. ↑ \"Exclusive: OpenAI co-founder Sutskever's new safety-focused AI startup SSI raises $1 billion\". \"OpenAI's former chief scientist is starting a new AI company\".","score":0.77199864,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.builtinsf.com/articles/safe-superintelligence-raises-2b-32b-valuation-20250415","title":"AI Innovator Safe Superintelligence Raises $2B at $32B Valuation | Built In San Francisco","content":"The investment will advance research and development and help SSI expand its global operations. Co-founded by Ilya Sutskever, former chief scientist at OpenAI, Safe Superintelligence is a company focused on developing superintelligent AI technology that is intentionally safe for the humans using it, according to reporting by Tech Funding News. With its tech still under development, SSI is working to ensure its systems incorporate alignment and control by design, rather than as a late additive.
